description:
You are an infrastructure engineer automating the preparation of “golden-image”
  configuration files for a provisioning pipeline.  A small file-tree full of
  *.template files has already been staged under /home/user/provision/templates/.
  Your assignment is to transform those templates into ready-to-use *.conf files,
  archive them, and produce a build log that our CI system can verify.

  Initial directory layout (already present before you start):

  /home/user/provision/
  ├── templates/
  │   ├── app/
  │   │   └── app.conf.template
  │   ├── db/
  │   │   └── db.conf.template
  │   └── web/
  │       └── nginx.conf.template
  └── (everything else is created by you)

  Step goals
  1. Recursively locate every file whose name ends with “.template” under
     /home/user/provision/templates/.
  2. Convert each one into a *.conf file by
     a) stripping the trailing “.template” extension and  
     b) **copying** the file into a new directory
        /home/user/provision/output/  (create it if it doesn’t exist).  
        Do **not** preserve the nested directory structure; place all finished
        *.conf files directly in /home/user/provision/output/.
  3. After every file has been copied, create a compressed archive named
     /home/user/provision/config_bundle.tar.gz
     that contains exactly the newly produced *.conf files at its top level
     (no sub-directories inside the archive).
  4. Produce a build log at /home/user/provision/build.log
     that records what was done.  The file **must match this exact format** so
     the automated test can perform a plain-text comparison:

        • One line per processed file, in alphabetical order by the
          source path (relative to /home/user/provision).  
        • A two–digit, zero-based index followed by a colon, one space,
          then the source relative path, one space, “->”, one space, and finally
          the absolute destination path.  
        • After the last file line, add a summary line
          TOTAL=<number_of_files>

     Example of the required syntax (actual order and paths will be the same
     in your case):

         00: templates/app/app.conf.template -> /home/user/provision/output/app.conf
         01: templates/db/db.conf.template  -> /home/user/provision/output/db.conf
         02: templates/web/nginx.conf.template -> /home/user/provision/output/nginx.conf
         TOTAL=3

     Important:  
     • There must be **no extra blank lines**, no trailing spaces, and the
       destination paths must be absolute.  
     • The index is zero-based, padded to two digits.  
     • Exactly one space on either side of “->”.

  Constraints & tips
  • Use the standard GNU toolchain available in a typical Linux environment.
  • Combine find, sort, xargs (or -exec) and tar so everything happens through
    shell commands—no manual edits.  
  • Do not modify the contents of the template files; just copy and rename them.
  • Ensure /home/user/provision/output/ ends up containing only the three *.conf
    files and nothing else.

  Completion criteria (all must be satisfied):
  a) Directory /home/user/provision/output/ exists and holds:
        app.conf, db.conf, nginx.conf
     with byte-for-byte identical contents to their .template counterparts.
  b) /home/user/provision/config_bundle.tar.gz exists and, when extracted,
     yields exactly those three *.conf files at the archive root.
  c) /home/user/provision/build.log matches the strict format described above.
  d) No residual *.template files appear in /home/user/provision/output/.

  The automated grader will untar, diff, and cat files to confirm success, so
  pay close attention to paths, naming, and log formatting.
truth:
Initial files (pre-created for the agent):

  1. /home/user/provision/templates/app/app.conf.template
     Contents (including trailing newline):
       APP_ENV=production
       PORT=8080

  2. /home/user/provision/templates/db/db.conf.template
     Contents (including trailing newline):
       DB_HOST=localhost
       DB_PORT=5432

  3. /home/user/provision/templates/web/nginx.conf.template
     Contents (including trailing newline):
       server {
           listen 80;
           server_name example.com;
       }

  Expected artefacts after successful agent execution:

  A. Directory /home/user/provision/output/ with permissions 755 containing:

     /home/user/provision/output/app.conf
         (exact byte copy of template #1 above)

     /home/user/provision/output/db.conf
         (exact byte copy of template #2 above)

     /home/user/provision/output/nginx.conf
         (exact byte copy of template #3 above)

  B. Archive /home/user/provision/config_bundle.tar.gz
     When unpacked into an empty directory, “tar -tzf” must list exactly:
       app.conf
       db.conf
       nginx.conf

  C. Log file /home/user/provision/build.log
     Exact content (with UNIX newlines and no trailing blank line):

       00: templates/app/app.conf.template -> /home/user/provision/output/app.conf
       01: templates/db/db.conf.template -> /home/user/provision/output/db.conf
       02: templates/web/nginx.conf.template -> /home/user/provision/output/nginx.conf
       TOTAL=3

  The test harness will:
  • Verify the three *.conf files exist and their SHA-256 checksums match the originals.
  • Confirm /home/user/provision/output/ contains nothing else.
  • Run “tar -tzf config_bundle.tar.gz” and compare the listing to the expected one.
  • Compare build.log byte-for-byte to the string above.
name: task_000000_ec19deb6
